evals.prompt: I want an external coding agent to review my staged changes, but I haven't decided whether to use Cursor or Claude Code yet. Help me run the review.
evals.expected_output: The skill should stop and ask the user which coding agent to use, because this methodology cannot execute without an explicitly named coding agent.

evals.prompt: Use Cursor to review this branch diff. I only care about regression risk, concurrency issues, and whether anything obvious was already checked in the previous review pass.
evals.expected_output: The skill should structure the review method around scope locking, background, already-reviewed facts, findings-first output, unique review artifacts, and consulting Cursor's own operating guide for the concrete invocation details.

evals.prompt: Use my team's custom coding agent wrapper to review these three files. Keep it review-only, do not patch anything, and make sure the run can be repeated safely in parallel with another review worker.
evals.expected_output: The skill should require a dedicated coding-agent operating guide, preserve the review-only boundary, require unique per-run artifacts and run ids, and return a methodology that separates review execution from any later patch/build/test phase.
